tavily.note: documented default config; answer not enabled (null by default)

generation.topup_note: if duplicates + the gate exhaust the decks before scale.topics is reached, a top-up round is generated by the same mechanism — fresh lists published in candidates<n>.json, draw seeded by their hash. Repeated until N. No human choice.
generation.draw_note: each model produces its candidates in one call, without seeing the others and with no domain instruction whatsoever. The harness randomly draws picks_per_model topics per list; seed = SHA-256 of candidates.json — chosen by nobody, verifiable by everyone. A duplicate (mechanical normalization) or a candidate with no recent news: skipped, the draw continues.
generation.dedup_note: final duplicate check: one call, the numbered list of retained topics. The mechanical rule misses cross-language duplicates; this model can only FLAG numbers (never choose a topic) — replacements come from the draw. All its verdicts are published in gate.json.

anchor.note: independent truth anchor: neither us nor the adversary. Cached by day+question, never paid for twice.

judging.reproduce: single judge. The guarantee against judge bias is not a second judge chosen by us, but public re-judging: `node bin/4-judge.mjs runs/<date> --model=X` replays everything with the model of your choice, on our raw payloads.
judging.blinding: mako sits in position A when the question index is even, B otherwise

excluded_from_payload.note: the SYNTHESES of both vendors are out of scope. Nothing else is removed: the payload ships verbatim.

token_counting: the ENTIRE JSON returned by the API — that is what the agent passes to its LLM, hence what it pays for. Counting only the `content` field matches no actual invoice.
latency_counting: two latencies published side by side. wall: clocked by us around the HTTP call, same machine and same moment for both arms — it includes the network of the collecting machine. server: self-reported by each vendor inside its own payload (response_time_ms for SERPdive, response_time for Tavily) — network excluded, and without us ever clocking the adversary. Both are extracted from the published payloads, hence recomputable by the reader.
